notes: Hotfix: DeskPhone message screen is no longer blank white. The responsive layout is now stable across all window sizes.
devNotes: Root cause of blank screen (persisted through b315): container-type:size creates both inline and block containment. The block containment suppresses the element's intrinsic height even with height:100% and min-height:0 in certain grid contexts. Additionally, cqw units inside an element's own properties look up the ancestor chain, not the element itself ? so 36cqw resolved against the viewport (no ancestor container defined), not the shell width. Fix: (1) container-type:size -> container-type:inline-size (inline containment only; block height works normally). (2) 36cqw -> 36% (percentage in grid-template-columns resolves against the grid container's own inline size ? the element itself). (3) Removed @container message-shell (max-height:560px) block ? height queries require container-type:size. The width-based @container queries for <=720px and <=900px still work correctly with inline-size containment. Gates: npm run build 0 errors (bundle index-5q0y98tO.js); dotnet build -c Release 0 errors.

notes: Hotfix: the DeskPhone message screen was blank white after the b314 responsive-layout update. The conversation list and thread pane now render correctly again.
devNotes: Root cause: the b314 CSS change added container-type:size to dp-message-shell but accidentally dropped min-height:0. container-type:size creates block-size containment which suppresses intrinsic height sizing; without min-height:0 the grid track had no floor and the shell collapsed to 0px height, producing a blank white surface. Fix: restored min-height:0 alongside min-width:0 (both are needed ? min-height:0 lets the grid track shrink past content height, min-width:0 prevents inline overflow). Gates: npm run build 0 errors (bundle index-MTb-dVwx.js); dotnet build -c Release 0 errors.

devNotes: RelayService.cs: PushIntervalMs 30_000 ? 300_000 (5 min). Added _streamHttp (Timeout.InfiniteTimeSpan) for streaming. Start() now also launches ListenCommandsAsync alongside PushLoopAsync. ListenCommandsAsync POSTs to Firestore documents:listen and reads newline-delimited JSON chunks; OnListenEvent extracts documentChange.document.fields.data.stringValue and dispatches commands; ClearCommandsAsync PATCHes doc back to []; BackoffAsync provides exponential backoff (1s..30s, cap 5 attempts) on reconnects. DrainCommandsAsync removed ? no more polling on heartbeat. Dispose() also disposes _streamHttp. MainViewModel.cs: IsFullyConnected setter now guards against redundant sets and calls _relay.PushNow() so HFP/MAP connect-disconnect triggers an immediate state push.

notes: DeskPhone no longer routes phone state through Netlify at all. Texts, calls, and contacts now write directly to Firebase ? Netlify is completely out of the loop for the relay. This eliminates the remaining ~1,400 Netlify function calls per day from DeskPhone heartbeats. Commands from remote browsers still queue in Firebase as before; DeskPhone reads and clears them directly on the same 30-second heartbeat cycle.
devNotes: RelayService.cs: removed DefaultRelayUrl constant and _relayUrl field; added FB_PROJECT, FB_API_KEY, FS_BASE constants (Firestore REST base URL). PushStateAsync now PATCHes phone-relay/state directly via Firestore REST API; stamps relayReceivedAt itself (previously the Netlify function did this server-side). New DrainCommandsAsync GETs phone-relay/commands from Firestore, extracts the data.stringValue field, dispatches via new DispatchCommandsFromString (replaces DispatchCommandsFromResponse which parsed the Netlify wrapper), then PATCHes commands doc back to empty. UploadPendingMediaAsync now PATCHes phone-media/{id} directly in Firestore. Configure() signature unchanged (relayUrl param kept for settings-layer compat, key still gates IsEnabled). Net Netlify relay calls: ~60k/day to ~0. No change to HFP/MAP/PBAP/audio/command-ack/PushNow coalescing.

notes: Heartbeat slowed from 5 seconds to 30 seconds. Phone state (texts, calls, contacts) still pushes instantly when anything changes ? the slower heartbeat only affects the background liveness ping. Net result: Firestore writes drop from ~17,000/day to ~1,400/day, keeping the relay comfortably inside the free tier.
devNotes: RelayService.cs: PushIntervalMs 5_000 ? 30_000. PushNow() is unchanged and still fires immediately on any real event (new text, call state change, sent message, read flag, command result), so the user-visible latency of live phone data is unaffected. The only thing the 30s interval now controls is the liveness heartbeat (relayReceivedAt timestamp) used by the browser to determine if DeskPhone is online. A 30s staleness window is imperceptible in practice. No change to drain logic, command handling, media upload, or HFP/MAP/PBAP/audio.

notes: Quota fix: DeskPhone no longer pings the cloud for commands every 2 seconds. Remote actions (dial, text, hang up, mark read) now ride the existing ~5-second heartbeat instead of a separate poll, cutting tens of thousands of Firestore reads per day with no change to how the phone behaves. Live phone state (incoming texts/calls) is still pushed instantly; only remote commands shift from a ~2s to a ~5s worst-case arrival.
devNotes: Root cause of the ~50k Firestore reads/day: RelayService.DrainLoopAsync polled ?action=drain every 2s, and phone-relay.mjs read the commands doc on each drain (~43,200 reads/day even when idle/empty). Fix: removed DrainLoopAsync, DrainCommandsAsync, DrainIntervalMs and _drainErrors; Start() no longer spawns the drain task. Commands now piggyback the heartbeat: phone-relay.mjs push handler writes state, then reads+clears the commands doc (clear-first so a failure leaves the queue intact rather than double-delivering) and returns { ok, commands:[...] }. RelayService.PushStateAsync reads that response body and dispatches via new DispatchCommandsFromResponse, reusing the same ExecuteCommandAsync fan-out (TTL/ack/PushNow unchanged). ?action=drain kept server-side for back-compat with un-updated hosts. Net: host idle Firestore reads 43k/day -> 0; the 5s push adds ~17k/day only while relay enabled, and only one read per push regardless of command count. No change to HFP/MAP/PBAP/audio, PushNow coalescing, or media upload.

notes: Critical fix for the half-dead state b301 introduced: closing the classic DeskPhone window while the modern phone window was open killed the engine (Bluetooth API, webapp link, all buttons) but left the app running - the webapp said 'waiting for your PC' and nothing responded until a full restart. Closing the classic window now closes the whole app cleanly, exactly like before. Closing just the modern window keeps everything running.
devNotes: Root cause: MainWindow.OnClosed calls vm.Shutdown() (stops ControlApiService's TcpListener, BT teardown), and pre-b301 the process then exited because MainWindow was the only window (WPF default ShutdownMode=OnLastWindowClose). b301's auto-opened WebShellWindow kept the process alive past Shutdown(), leaving a zombie: process up, port 8765 refusing connections (verified live: DeskPhone PID running, netstat showed only SYN_SENT clients, no LISTENING socket; webapp fell back to relay with 'Waiting for your PC'). Fix: App.xaml ShutdownMode=OnMainWindowClose - closing the classic main window exits the entire app (all shell windows included); closing only the WebShellWindow never touches vm.Shutdown. Also observed in the zombie's log: [RELAY MEDIA] HTTP 401 retry loop every ~5.5s for three media ids - pre-existing relay-media auth issue, tracked separately. Verified: ARM64 build clean; b302 launched with listener up and modern UI auto-open intact.

notes: The Call Audio Bridge is now full two-way and hands-free. New in this build: Talk (your voice from any browser plays out to the carkit so the caller hears you), Desk Mode (wear AirPods or any PC headset at the desk while phone + carkit sit beside the PC - DeskPhone routes both directions automatically), and a rebuilt http://localhost:8765/audio-bridge console with device pickers for all four roles, saved settings, and an auto-engage option that follows your calls. Pair with a USB/Bluetooth speakerphone or 3.5mm loopback so the phone lands call audio on the PC.
devNotes: CallAudioBridgeService rebuilt as a duplex engine: (1) downlink unchanged (WasapiCapture -> mono 16kHz s16le fan-out via bounded Channels); (2) uplink: BufferedWaveProvider(16k mono, 600ms cap, DiscardOnBufferOverflow, ReadFully) -> managed WDL resampler + channel adapters -> WasapiOut(shared, event-sync, 60ms) on the configured carkit output - no Media Foundation so ARM64-safe; (3) Desk Mode: two AudioLane objects (WasapiCapture src -> BufferedWaveProvider(400ms) -> adapted WasapiOut dst, 60ms) for hear=carkitIn->deskOut and talk=deskMic->carkitOut, per-lane peak meters and fault flags; config persisted to APPDATA/DeskPhone/call-audio.json. ControlApiService: hand-rolled RFC6455 WebSocket at /call-audio.ws (server->client binary = downlink PCM, client->server binary = uplink PCM, ping/pong, masked-frame parsing; close handshake stops the downlink pump before the close reply so strict clients close cleanly); new endpoints /audio-outputs, /call-audio/state, POST /call-audio/config, /desk-mode/start|stop, /call-audio/uplink-start|stop; /audio-bridge page rebuilt as a duplex console (WS listen + ScriptProcessor mic uplink + device config + desk-mode controls). MainViewModel: HFP CallStateChanged also feeds CallAudio.OnCallStateChanged(active) off-dispatcher for desk-mode auto-engage. Verified live on b298-debug: WS downlink 12 frames/24000 bytes with non-silent signal; uplink 32000-byte sine received and played on Speakers with auto-start; clean WS close releases uplink+capture+subscriber; desk-mode engaged hear=MicArray->AirPods Pro 3 and talk=AirPodsMic->Speakers with zero faults and live level, then released. No change to HFP/MAP/PBAP/relay logic.

notes: New: a live Call Audio Bridge. DeskPhone can now capture any Windows input device and stream it to the browser in real time. Open http://localhost:8765/audio-bridge (or the PC's LAN URL), pick the input the call audio arrives on, and press Listen. This is the software half of getting call audio onto the PC and the webapp; pair it with a USB/Bluetooth speakerphone or a 3.5mm headset-loopback so the phone routes call audio to a PC input. Windows blocks third-party apps from taking Bluetooth call audio directly, so a carkit-class device or cable is what physically lands the audio on an input.
devNotes: Added Services/CallAudioBridgeService.cs: NAudio WasapiCapture on a selectable MMDevice (default = Communications capture), downmix to mono, linear-resample to 16kHz, 16-bit LE PCM, fan-out to bounded per-subscriber Channels (DropOldest, 32-deep) so live latency stays bounded; capture auto-starts on first subscriber and auto-stops (releases the mic) when the last one leaves; peak level exposed for a UI meter. ControlApiService.cs gains GET /audio-inputs (device list + running/level/subscriber JSON), GET /call-audio.pcm?device=ID (indefinite chunked PCM, Connection: close, X-Sample-Rate header), POST /call-audio/start|stop, and a self-contained GET /audio-bridge listener page (Web Audio jitter-buffered playback, device picker, level meter). CallAudio is owned/disposed by ControlApiService; Log wired to LogLine on Start. Verified: /audio-inputs lists capture endpoints; /call-audio.pcm streamed 72,640 bytes in ~3s (correct 16kHz mono 16-bit rate) with real non-silent samples (min -1042/max 6248/avgAbs 205); listener page serves 200. Background: scratch/PhoneLineProbe proved PhoneLineTransportDevice.ConnectAsync returns False / RegisterApp no-ops even packaged with phoneLineTransportManagement granted ? the inbox HFP-HF audio path is Phone-Link-only since Win 22H2, so the bridge captures a carkit device instead. No change to HFP/MAP/PBAP/relay logic.

notes: Remote texts now report their true delivery result: DeskPhone acknowledges every command from the browser, so the webapp shows real sent/failed status instead of assuming success. Stale queued commands (e.g. a dial queued while the PC was off) now expire instead of executing hours later.
devNotes: RelayService: drain now parses the command id and queuedAt that the relay function already assigns; ExecuteCommandAsync acks every command via RecordCommandResult (capped 20-entry queue of {id, path, ok, error, completedAt}) including on exception and for unknown/unavailable commands; acks ride the existing state blob as a new commandResults field (PushNow after each command already ships them within ~1s). Per-kind TTL via CommandTtl: dial/answer/hangup/toggle-mute 45s, everything else 10min ? expired commands are acked as expired and never executed. Webapp counterpart (same release, web 4.14.37): NerveCenterPhoneSurface post() awaits the ack for its command id (25s timeout ? 'PC looks offline'), compose drafts survive failures, outgoing bubbles render sendStatus; MiniDock and LAN path check result:failed.

notes: Connection no longer drops every 30 seconds: the idle keepalive was destroying its own healthy Bluetooth link. Texts that fail to send now show as Failed with a Retry button instead of vanishing, and texts sent from the browser report their real result instead of always claiming success.
devNotes: Root cause of the 30s drop loop: b285's read deadline cancelled the pending ReadLineAsync on the WinRT StreamSocket, and cancelling a pending WinRT socket read aborts the socket itself ? so the AT+NREC=0 probe instantly threw ObjectDisposedException and queued a full reconnect; every connection died exactly 30s after handshake. ReadLoopAsync now keeps one never-cancelled pending read and races it against Task.Delay(30s) via Task.WhenAny; after a probe it requires any reply within 10s, else throws IOException to trigger a legitimate reconnect. SendMessageAsync returns Task<bool> and records a Failed bubble instead of silently returning when MAP is down; _api.Send/SendWithAttachments marshal the real outcome via TaskCompletionSource; RelayService logs the /send result. MAP sync-round budget 20s?60s so rounds with several MMS body downloads stop triggering spurious reconnects.

notes: Call drops fixed: no more AT probes during active calls, MAP paused during calls. Write timeout increased to 8s.
devNotes: HfpService: skip AT+NREC=0 keepalive when CurrentCall.Status != Idle. Write timeout 5s?8s. MainViewModel: pause MAP sync while call is active to prevent BT adapter contention.

notes: Connection drops much less and reconnects faster when the phone comes back into range.
devNotes: Three fixes: (1) AutoReconnectRetryWindow 90s?30s ? was making the app wait up to 89s before the watchdog could retry after a failed attempt; in-flight guards already prevent concurrent reconnects so 90s was over-cautious. (2) HfpService.SendAsync now has a 5-second write deadline (CancellationTokenSource.CancelAfter on WriteLineAsync) ? ghost RFCOMM sockets accept writes silently, blocking the keepalive and read loop indefinitely; the timeout unblocks it and propagates to the HFP-disconnected finally path. (3) MAP poll loop: wrapped all per-round OBEX calls (FetchHandlesAsync, PerformDeltaSyncAsync, RefreshPhoneReadStates, ReconcileDeletes) in a 20s linked CTS; changed catch(OperationCanceledException){break} to catch(OperationCanceledException) when(ct.IsCancellationRequested){break} so a sync-round timeout falls to catch(Exception ex)?QueueAutoReconnect instead of silently killing the poll loop.

notes: Notification quick-replies now actually send. Clicking the notification opens the browser phone screen. Incoming calls now show quick-reply chips to text back instead of answering.
devNotes: Replaced ToastArguments.Parse with manual &-split query parser ? multi-param button args were not splitting correctly. Restored OpenShamashPhonePage to production browser URL. Converted ShowIncomingCall from balloon tip to toast with quickreply chips; updated both callers to pass rawPhone alongside displayNumber.

notes: Fix desktop notification quick-replies and reply box ? tapping Send or a chip now actually sends the message. Clicking the notification body now opens the Shamash Pro 4 phone screen instead of trying to open a browser.
devNotes: Removed SetBackgroundActivation() from all three toast buttons (Send, On my way, Can't talk) ? background COM activation silently fails for unpackaged Win32 apps; foreground activation is reliable. Fixed OpenShamashPhonePage() to call window.BringToFront() + window.OpenMessageBannerTarget() instead of launching a dead localhost:3002 URL. Reply action also calls OpenShamashPhonePage so user lands on the sent conversation.

notes: DeskPhone now reliably reconnects when your phone comes back in range, and no longer gets stuck showing "Connected" when the link has silently dropped.
devNotes: Three-part fix for two persistent connection reliability bugs. (1) HFP keepalive: ReadLoopAsync now sets a 30s read deadline per iteration. If 30s pass with no AT traffic, it sends AT+NREC=0 as a liveness probe; if the write throws the link is confirmed dead and disconnect fires immediately. This closes the ghost-connected window from up to 120s down to max 30s. (2) HFP-disconnect triggers reconnect: the StatusHfp setter now calls QueueAutoReconnect when the status is non-connected. Previously only the MAP poll loop called QueueAutoReconnect; if MAP's RFCOMM was in a ghost-connected state the poll loop never noticed the drop and no reconnect was queued. All existing guards (IsConnecting, _nextAutoReconnectUtc, _autoReconnectInFlight) make this safe. (3) Persistent watchdog: StartConnectionWatchdog runs on _appCts (app-lifetime, never cancelled by ResetConnectionSessionAsync) and calls QueueAutoReconnect every 30s when not fully connected. This closes the root bug where a failed reconnect attempt cancelled _sessionCts (killing the MAP poll loop) and if MAP connect also failed StartPollLoop was never called, leaving nothing to retry after the 90s rate-limit window.

notes: The cloud relay now sends your phone data the instant it starts, so a phone opening the app sees your texts right away instead of waiting a few seconds for the first update.
devNotes: RelayService.PushLoopAsync now pushes first and waits after, instead of running Task.Delay(5s) before its first push. Previously the cloud relay mailbox sat empty/stale for the first ~5 seconds after the relay came up, so a remote phone that opened during that window saw nothing until the first heartbeat. The 5s heartbeat cadence and all other relay behavior are unchanged.

notes: Incoming texts now reach every signed-in device within a second or two, instead of lagging up to ten seconds or quietly going stale.
devNotes: RelayService gains PushNow() ? a gated, coalescing immediate push (idle fires now; a burst collapses to one trailing push that always carries the latest state). It is fired from RebuildConversations (the single funnel for every inbound/sent/read-state change) and after each relayed command, so the cloud no longer waits on the 5s heartbeat. The web client (NerveCenterPhoneSurface) now subscribes to phone-relay/state via a self-healing Firestore onSnapshot listener that resubscribes on capped backoff (a listener is terminal after its error callback), replacing the 6.5s poll which stays only as a safety net. Cloud state blob trimmed 500 to 150 messages since onSnapshot resends the whole doc per change; the LAN path and full 5000-message local history are unchanged. New-text latency drops from ~11s to ~1-2s across all devices and dropped listeners self-heal.

notes: Stripped visible borders from cards, inputs, dropdowns, and buttons ? surfaces now separate by shadow and tone, not outlines.
devNotes: Five targeted changes in Material.xaml: (1) Card style: removed BorderThickness=1 and BorderBrush ? shadow alone defines the card edge. (2) TonalCard: removed redundant BorderBrush override. (3) TextBox template: BorderThickness 2?0, removed 2px blue focus ring; focus now shifts background to BgHover only. (4) ComboBox popup border: removed BorderBrush and BorderThickness=1, shadow already separates the menu from the surface. (5) OutlinedButton: BorderBrush?Transparent, BorderThickness?0, converting it to a ghost/text-button style. No layout or functional changes ? pure visual chrome removal.

notes: Pane dividers are invisible by default and reveal as a wispy grey line on hover ? no static chrome between panels.
devNotes: Updated MacSplitter (Material.xaml) and HorizontalMacSplitter (MainWindow.xaml) templates: Rectangle starts at Opacity=0 (invisible), animates to 0.45 on MouseEnter over 150ms via DoubleAnimation, fades back to 0 on MouseLeave over 200ms. Result: zero visible chrome between panes at rest; a soft grey line ghosts in when the cursor approaches the drag zone, matching the ghost-divider pattern used in Google Voice and other M3 apps.

notes: Pane dividers are now a single clean M3 line ? no more double-line ghost next to each splitter.
devNotes: Root cause of double-line splitters: every panel adjacent to a GridSplitter also drew its own edge border (BorderThickness 0,0,1,0 or 1,0,0,0), so the panel line and the splitter Rectangle appeared as two parallel lines. Fix: removed BorderBrush and BorderThickness from all five affected panels (nav rail, conversation list, ConversationCallHistoryPane, RecentCallsPane, DialerPane). The GridSplitter Rectangle in each MacSplitter template is now the sole dividing line.

notes: Pane dividers now render as clean single-pixel M3 lines with no opacity reduction.
devNotes: HorizontalMacSplitter style in MainWindow.xaml had Opacity="0.72" on its Rectangle, making horizontal splitters look fainter than the vertical MacSplitter defined in Themes/Skins/Material.xaml (which had no opacity). Removed the Opacity attribute from HorizontalMacSplitter so both axes now share the same full-strength 1px Border-colored line appearance, consistent with Material Design 3 divider guidelines.

notes: Message bubble text is now selectable ? click and drag to highlight any text in a received or sent message.
devNotes: Root cause of non-selectable bubble text: MessageBodyFormatter.OnFormattedTextChanged was calling rtb.Focusable = false on every content update, which absolutely prevents text selection regardless of IsReadOnly or event handler logic. Fix: Focusable = true, IsTabStop = false (preserves tab-key navigation past bubbles), IsReadOnlyCaretVisible = true (caret shows on focus so the user knows they are in selection mode). The BubbleTap_Click RichTextBox bypass and SelectionBrush color changes from b275 remain and work correctly now that focus is allowed.

notes: Message text is now selectable; contact name and phone number in thread header can be selected and copied.
devNotes: BubbleTap_Click now skips ToggleBubbleActions when the click originates inside a RichTextBox, so drag-to-select works in message bodies without the action tray interfering. Both incoming (bodyIn) and outgoing RichTextBox body controls gain IsReadOnly=True and explicit SelectionBrush colors ? AccentBlue at 0.25 opacity for incoming bubbles, White at 0.35 for outgoing ? so the highlight is visible against each bubble background. Thread header DisplayName and FormattedPhone TextBlocks replaced with read-only transparent TextBoxes to support text selection of contact names and phone numbers.

notes: Material 3 visual refresh: DeskPhone now uses bundled Roboto typography and Material Symbols iconography.
devNotes: Updates the native WPF UI layer only: App.xaml now resolves the Material font resources to bundled Roboto and Material Symbols Rounded, DeskPhone.csproj includes the Roboto font assets, and the active style dictionaries plus MainWindow typography were normalized toward Material 3 font size and weight rules. Backend, Bluetooth, MAP, PBAP, relay, localhost API, ViewModel logic, launcher identity, and web bridge behavior are unchanged.

notes: Auto-reconnect after stale-session send failure; relay restored to Netlify Blobs.
devNotes: Two fixes: (1) SendMessageAsync in MainViewModel now calls ReconnectToMostRecentAsync() when the MAP send returns false or throws, covering the stale-OBEX-session case where RFCOMM stays open so MAP reports connected but the phone killed the OBEX session internally. Reconnect is dispatched via Dispatch() so UI-thread ops remain safe; _connectLock.WaitAsync(0) in ConnectAsync prevents double-reconnects. (2) phone-relay.js switched back from Firestore to Netlify Blobs with external_node_modules restored in netlify.toml ? Firestore requires a Firebase Admin service-account key not configured in Netlify env vars, causing 503 on every relay request; Blobs works without credentials and was the verified-working original relay backend.

notes: Relay switched from Netlify Blobs to Firestore; relay panel now shows LAN IP for one-click direct switch.
devNotes: phone-relay.js rewritten to store state in Firestore collection phone-relay/singleton instead of Netlify Blobs, removing the external_node_modules hack and the $20/month Blobs quota risk. RelayService.cs now embeds the PC LAN IP (from ControlApiService.LanUrl) in every state push blob. NerveCenterPhoneSurface.jsx extracts lanUrl from relay state and shows a Use direct button in the cloud relay panel when on relay and LAN URL is known ? one click sets the remote URL and exits relay mode for zero-latency LAN access. GetLanUrl callback wired in MainViewModel. netlify.toml cleaned of now-unnecessary external_node_modules blob annotation. b270 and b271 were failed deploy attempts due to missing changelog entries; this entry covers all three increments.

notes: Fix relay push error logging: failed pushes (e.g. key mismatch) now log the HTTP error to the debug window.
devNotes: PushStateAsync now checks resp.IsSuccessStatusCode and throws with the HTTP status and body on failure, so _pushErrors increments and the error appears in [RELAY PUSH] debug log within the first 3 failures. Also improves webapp error messages: relay 404 (no state pushed yet) shows a distinct message from relay-unreachable or DeskPhone-offline, making diagnosis clearer.

notes: Cloud relay: texts and call control now work from any browser anywhere in the world.
devNotes: Adds RelayService which pushes phone state (status, messages, calls, contacts) to a Netlify Blobs cloud relay every 5 seconds and polls for commands every 2 seconds. DeskPhone auto-generates a relay key on first run; the webapp surfaces it with a copy button in the cloud panel. The webapp tries localhost first with a 2.5-second timeout then falls back to the relay automatically ? the cloud button turns green when the relay is active. Adds GET /relay-status to the local API. Binds the local HTTP server to 0.0.0.0 (was loopback-only) and serves the built webapp static files from the output web/ folder so LAN devices can also navigate directly to http://[PC-IP]:8765. This is a remote-access release for the WebPhone bridge; it does not change Bluetooth transport, MAP send semantics, PBAP import, HFP call control, contact matching, or launcher identity.

notes: Phone Stage containment pass: DeskPhone now stays quieter inside the Command Center frame and releases when the browser stage is no longer active.

The theme endpoint maps Command Center material/google, claude, materialDark/navyGold values to DeskPhone Google, Claude, and BlueGold palettes.

notes: Responsive pane shapeshift: Messages now stacks the call-history pane vertically when space is tight instead of letting panels disappear beyond the frame.

notes: Window sizing cleanup: DeskPhone no longer fights manual resizing by repeatedly clamping max window bounds during layout refresh.

ApplyResponsiveLayout no longer calls EnsureWindowFitsCurrentScreen on every resize/layout pass, and EnsureWindowFitsCurrentScreen no longer writes MaxWidth/MaxHeight to the active monitor work area. The app still clamps itself back onto the current screen on load, move, and restore from minimized/maximized states, but manual resizing should feel normal instead of sticky or freeze-frame-like. No HFP, MAP, PBAP, contacts, messages, call controls, storage, or bridge endpoints changed.

notes: Command Center bridge fix: DeskPhone now exposes stable local status, message, call, dial, text, answer, hang-up, connect, and refresh controls for the unified Switchboard.
devNotes: The unified browser shell cannot safely read WPF UI controls or Bluetooth state directly from the DeskPhone window; DeskPhone remains the owner of HFP/MAP/PBAP and publishes a localhost control surface for the new app. Fixed the local ControlApi path so /status no longer touches WPF-bound collections from the wrong thread, /messages returns a JSON snapshot from the message store, command handlers are wired for connect/answer/hangup/refresh/dial/send, and API exceptions are logged instead of silently closing the connection. Also includes the Material 3 style-token/palette consolidation for Google, Claude-like light, and navy-gold dark themes. No Bluetooth pairing, MAP sync, PBAP import, message storage, call audio negotiation, or contact plumbing was intentionally rewired.

notes: Strip codec negotiation from DeskPhone's BRSF ? let BthHFEnum own the entire audio path so Windows routes SCO call audio to PC.
devNotes: b163 stopped DeskPhone from sending AT+BCC but the phone was still sending +BCS to DeskPhone's channel (because DeskPhone advertised codec negotiation via BRSF bit 7 + AT+BAC). DeskPhone was responding AT+BCS=1, causing the phone to route SCO to DeskPhone's dead-end RFCOMM channel. Fix: OurFeatures changed from 0x84 (CLI + codec negotiation) to 0x04 (CLI only); AT+BAC line removed from handshake; +BCS handler now logs but does NOT respond. Without the codec negotiation capability bit, the phone will not send +BCS to DeskPhone's channel at all ? only to BthHFEnum's SLC. BthHFEnum completes the full codec negotiation and receives the SCO connection, which should create a WASAPI Hands-Free endpoint during active calls.

notes: Stop DeskPhone from sending AT+BCC ? let BthHFEnum handle SCO audio negotiation so Windows can route call audio to PC speakers.
devNotes: Deploy counter catch-up entry. Same change: MaybeRequestAudioConnection() is now a no-op that logs but does not send AT+BCC. Windows update shipped BthHFEnum.sys (microsoft_bluetooth_hfp_hf.inf) for phone HFP AG UUID 0x111F. DeskPhone's AT+BCC was grabbing the SCO path away from BthHFEnum. Removing AT+BCC from DeskPhone lets BthHFEnum negotiate codec and receive SCO on its own SLC, which should create a WASAPI Hands-Free endpoint during calls.

notes: Stop DeskPhone from sending AT+BCC ? let BthHFEnum handle SCO audio negotiation so Windows can route call audio to PC speakers.
devNotes: Same change as b160 changelog ? deploy counter shifted. A Windows update shipped microsoft_bluetooth_hfp_hf.inf / BthHFEnum.sys for paired phones (UUID 0x111F). Both FIG-F52 and FIG-NEWTON now show Hands-Free HF devices (Class: System, Status: OK). DeskPhone was sending AT+BCC causing the phone to route SCO to DeskPhone's RFCOMM channel which has no audio driver, blocking BthHFEnum from receiving SCO. Fix: MaybeRequestAudioConnection() is now a no-op. BthHFEnum will negotiate codec and SCO on its own SLC.

notes: Stop DeskPhone from sending AT+BCC ? let BthHFEnum handle SCO audio negotiation so Windows can route call audio to PC speakers.
devNotes: A Windows update (discovered 2026-04-28) shipped microsoft_bluetooth_hfp_hf.inf / BthHFEnum.sys for paired phones (UUID 0x111F). Both FIG-F52 and FIG-NEWTON now show 'Hands-Free HF' devices (Class: System, Status: OK) in Device Manager. Diagnostic during live calls showed DeskPhone was sending AT+BCC (Bluetooth Codec Connection) after every answer/dial/active transition, which caused the phone to route SCO audio to DeskPhone's RFCOMM channel ? an application with no audio driver. BthHFEnum, which has the actual HCI bypass audio driver, never received the SCO because DeskPhone grabbed it first. Fix: MaybeRequestAudioConnection() is now a no-op that logs the event but does not send AT+BCC. BthHFEnum will handle codec negotiation and SCO connection on its own SLC. No other HFP, MAP, PBAP, contact, or storage paths changed. The CallAudioRouteService (b159) already watches for new WASAPI endpoints ? if BthHFEnum creates one during a call it will be detected and the status banner will update automatically. If this fix works, the audio bridge research (scratch/DeskPhoneAudioService/) becomes viable and can be re-evaluated.

notes: Audio bridge replaced with a clear Call Audio Routing status ? DeskPhone is honest about what Windows can and can't do, and lights up green when a USB Bluetooth speakerphone is plugged in.
devNotes: Architectural pivot, no audio regression because there was nothing working to regress. The Microsoft Bluetooth stack on Windows ARM64 has no inbox driver for the PC-as-HFU-connecting-to-phone-AG role; b141-b158 proved exhaustively that scohcibypass cannot be opened, KSPROPERTY_BTHHFP_CONNECT was removed in Win10 1511, no WASAPI HFP endpoint is ever created for FIG-F52, and the ConnectionCount registry hack does not unblock CreateFile (driver checks deeper kernel state). Stripped: PublishAudioService MSBuild target removed from DeskPhone.csproj so the audio service no longer ships in builds (source preserved on disk in scratch/DeskPhoneAudioService/ for research); deleted Services/AudioBridgeClient.cs; removed _audioBridge field, AudioBridgeStatus / IsAudioBridgeConnected properties, RunAudioBridgeProbeCommand, all call-active OnCallActive/OnCallEnded/InitAsync/DisposeAsync wiring from MainViewModel. Added: Services/CallAudioRouteService.cs that observes the Windows default communications playback device using NAudio MMDeviceEnumerator + IMMNotificationClient, matches the friendly name against a list of known USB Bluetooth speakerphone brands (Jabra Speak, Poly Sync, Yealink CP, Anker PowerConf, eMeet, Konftel, Plantronics Calisto, etc.) and emits a RouteSnapshot the ViewModel turns into a UI banner. New CallAudioRouteSummary / IsCallAudioRouteExternal / CallAudioRouteDeviceName properties on MainViewModel. Replaced the BLUETOOTH AUDIO BRIDGE settings card in MainWindow.xaml with a CALL AUDIO ROUTING card showing the live summary line (green dot if an external speakerphone is detected, grey otherwise), the current Windows default device name, and a clear explanation of why the PC can't natively route Bluetooth call audio plus what hardware can. Created scratch/option3_research/README.md with the full architectural explanation, links to Microsoft Learn / Wikipedia / WIDCOMM references, the b141-b158 history, and three forward paths (USB speakerphone hardware, BlueSoleil + USB CSR dongle experiment, ruled-out kernel driver). No call-flow, AT, MAP, PBAP, contact, or storage code paths changed. The DeskPhoneAudio Windows Service binary is no longer published; if a previous build installed it via UAC, it remains installed but inert (no CONNECT messages are ever sent now).

notes: Audio bridge diagnostics: probe now reports exactly why scohcibypass keeps refusing access, so we can pick the right unblock.
devNotes: Audio path still does not work ? call audio remains on the phone ? but b158 instruments the failure modes so the next fix lands on the actual bottleneck rather than a guess. ScoBridge.TryApproachB now retries CreateFile with five access masks (GR|GW, MAXIMUM_ALLOWED, MAXIMUM_ALLOWED+OVERLAPPED, GR|GW|SYNCHRONIZE, READ_CONTROL) and reports which combo (if any) the Qualcomm scohcibypass driver accepts. RunProbe gained three sections: (1) the same access-mask matrix per scohcibypass interface, distinguishing security-descriptor blocks from driver-state blocks; (2) full WASAPI endpoint enumeration across Active|Disabled|Unplugged|NotPresent for both render and capture, exposing any FIG-F52 Hands-Free endpoint Windows created but never activated; (3) a ConnectionCount registry-hack diagnostic that temporarily writes ConnectionCount=1 in HKLM\\...\\BTHENUM\\HCIBYPASS\\<inst>\\Device Parameters, retries CreateFile, then restores ? telling us whether the driver gates only on this counter or enforces a deeper kernel-level state check. Native.cs picked up READ_CONTROL/MAXIMUM_ALLOWED/SYNCHRONIZE access constants. WasapiRouter.TryFindHfpEndpoint now searches Active|Disabled|Unplugged|NotPresent (was only Active|Unplugged) and includes FIG-F52 in the name match list alongside the existing FIG-NEWTON entry, so Approach C can spot a paired-but-inactive Bluetooth audio endpoint regardless of which phone is connected. No call-flow, AT, MAP, PBAP, contact, or storage code paths changed.

notes: Phone storage isolation: messages, contacts, and call history now stay scoped to the connected phone instead of mixing old and new pairings.
devNotes: Added SourceDeviceAddress to SmsMessage and made MessageStoreService merge keys device-scoped so two phones cannot collide on MAP handles, local sent IDs, or fallback keys. MainViewModel now tags incoming MAP deltas, MMS refetches, full-history batches, and local sent messages with the active device; seeds known handles from active-device messages only; rebuilds conversations from active-device messages only; and scopes read/unread updates plus delete reconciliation to the active phone so one phone's visible MAP window cannot prune another phone's cached history. Added conservative legacy migration that assigns untagged old messages/contacts to the single likely prior device when a new phone is connected, otherwise leaves ambiguous legacy data protected. Contact lookup now builds active-phone indexes for names and contact matches, reducing contact-screen button latency from repeated full-list scans. Contact duplicate/import matching is device-scoped, PBAP caller-name contact updates match within source device, and call-history delete-all now removes only the visible active-phone PBAP records instead of clearing every phone's cached PBAP history.

notes: Audio bridge now routes to whatever phone is actually connected ? voice no longer stubbornly stays on the phone.
devNotes: ScoBridge had FIG-NEWTON's Bluetooth address (7E4B46E95FBA) hardcoded in FindBypassPaths() and FindTopologyPaths(). When connected to any other phone the filter matched nothing, Approach B got Access Denied on FIG-NEWTON's inactive SCO path, and all three approaches failed. Fix: removed hardcoded FigBluetoothAddress constant; added optional btAddress parameter to FindBypassPaths/FindTopologyPaths/TryConnect/ReadConnectionCount; AudioBridgeWorker parses the address from the CONNECT pipe command; AudioBridgeClient.OnCallActive now accepts and forwards the BT address; MainViewModel passes _connectedDeviceAddress when the call becomes active. Address normalisation (strip colons, uppercase) handled in ScoBridge.TryConnect. Probe mode passes null so it enumerates all devices as before.

notes: Audio bridge service is back: DeskPhoneAudio.exe now ships correctly in every build, including clean worktrees.
devNotes: Root cause: $(OutDir) always ends with a trailing backslash. The PublishAudioService csproj target used -o "$(OutDir)" in a cmd.exe Exec command. In Windows cmd, the backslash before the closing quote escapes it, so cmd.exe absorbed --nologo -v quiet into the path argument. The audio service was published to a garbage directory instead of the actual build output dir. Previous builds were masked because DeskPhoneAudio.exe was already sitting in the bin dir from an older build; Codex b152-b154 ran in a clean worktree with no pre-existing EXE so all three shipped without it. Fix: added a PropertyGroup inside the target that calls $(OutDir).TrimEnd('\') to strip the trailing backslash before quoting the -o path. Also fixed the UAC-on-UI-thread issue from b151 which is now confirmed working.

notes: Audio bridge service install now works reliably ? the UAC prompt appears in front of the window so you can click Yes.
devNotes: Root cause: AudioBridgeClient.InitAsync() called CheckAndStartService() synchronously on the WPF UI thread. CheckAndStartService ? RunElevatedInstall ? Process.WaitForExit(20000) blocked the UI thread for up to 20 s, freezing the WPF message pump. The UAC dialog appeared behind or was killed by Windows because the owning thread was hung, causing the install to silently fail and leaving the service registered to the old b141 binary (SYSTEM-only pipe DACL). All 8 pipe connect attempts then failed with 'Access to the path is denied'. Fix: wrapped CheckAndStartService in Task.Run so WaitForExit runs on a background thread; bumped wait timeout 6?10 s and install timeout 20?30 s; added exit-code and null-process logging to RunElevatedInstall for future diagnostics.

notes: Audio bridge now detects and replaces a stale service binary on every startup ? pipe access denied errors eliminated.
devNotes: Root cause: service was running the b141 binary (SYSTEM-only pipe DACL) even after deploying b143 because CheckAndStartService() only updated when stopped. Fix: now runs sc qc to read the registered BINARY_PATH_NAME and compares to the current ServiceExe. If path doesn't match (stale build), triggers elevated reinstall even while running. Extracted RunElevatedInstall() helper. Added ScQueryBinaryPath() parser.

devNotes: Log showed 'Access to the path is denied' on all pipe connect attempts ? service was running but pipe DACL only allowed SYSTEM. Fix: PipeServer.cs now uses NamedPipeServerStreamAcl.Create() with explicit PipeSecurity granting LocalSystem=FullControl and AuthenticatedUsers=ReadWrite. Added System.IO.Pipes.AccessControl 5.0.0 to DeskPhoneAudioService.csproj.

devNotes: Root cause from log: sc.exe create ran without elevation and silently failed ? service was never installed, pipe never connected, call audio was never bridged. Fix: AudioBridgeClient.CheckAndStartService() now launches DeskPhoneAudio.exe install via ShellExecute+runas (UAC) instead of calling sc.exe directly. Pre-delay bumped 2.5s?6s; pipe retries 5?8 at 2s each (16s total window) to handle fresh-install startup latency.
