notes: Bluetooth call handling now explicitly asks the phone to hand the live hands-free voice path over to the PC during answer and dial flows instead of only controlling the call state.
devNotes: Updated HfpService so the app advertises codec-negotiation support, sends AT+BAC=1 during service-level setup, accepts the phone's later +BCS codec selection, and issues AT+BCC when a call is answered, dialed, or becomes active so the phone is explicitly asked to bring up the HF audio connection instead of leaving audio on the handset. Also fenced scratch probes, archived builds, artifacts, and message-export folders out of the main project file so normal repo builds stop ingesting workspace noise and can produce a clean rewindable release.

notes: The app now shifts onto the new Material 3 visual foundation, and Audio Settings now shows whether Windows has actually exposed your phone as a real call speaker and microphone path instead of pretending the issue is just a default-device choice.
devNotes: Updated the shared app styling foundation toward the Material 3 palette and control model across App resources, theme palettes, and shared button styling so the current visual-system pass ships in a rewindable build. Reworked DeskPhone audio inspection so AudioService now checks the phone's HFP, headset-gateway, and A2DP registrations directly from Windows, lists both playback and microphone communications endpoints, logs a structured audio-route diagnostic line, and reports when the phone is present for call control but Windows still has not materialized a usable call-audio endpoint. Added the new phone-audio investigation note under docs and corrected the HFP logging text so the app no longer implies it successfully created a call-audio driver path when it only issued a profile-enable request.

notes: Incoming ringtone playback now stops as soon as a ringing call is answered or otherwise leaves the ringing state, instead of continuing after pickup.
devNotes: Fixed a call-state aliasing bug between HfpService and MainViewModel by publishing cloned CallInfo snapshots instead of exposing the same mutable live object across events. That preserves the true transition from IncomingRinging to Active or Idle so the notification layer can reliably stop the ringtone. Also simplified the DeskPhone-side alert rule so any non-ringing call state immediately cancels the incoming-call alert path, covering both desktop answer and phone-side pickup.

notes: Pinned messages now appear as real entries in the right-side conversation pane, and clicking a pinned entry jumps back to that exact bubble in the thread instead of leaving message pinning as a buried icon only.
devNotes: Added selected-conversation pinned-message projections in MainViewModel so the UI can surface pinned message entries independently of the raw bubble list and refresh immediately when a message is pinned or unpinned. Expanded the Messages right-side detail pane with a dedicated pinned-messages section above the full call history, and wired those entries to scroll the thread to the target bubble and reopen that bubble's action tray so pinned messages behave like retrievable thread markers instead of static decorations.

notes: Message-bubble taps no longer throw a Windows document-tree error, plain bubble clicks open the in-place action tray safely, and the repo now hardcodes open-source messenger and calling apps as the standing benchmark for standard interaction behavior.
devNotes: Replaced the bubble-click ancestor walk with a document-safe parent resolver that handles WPF content elements such as Run and Hyperlink instead of assuming every click source lives in the visual tree. Bubble taps now ignore embedded hyperlinks as well as button hits so rich message-body actions keep working without opening the tray. Added durable benchmark instructions in AGENTS.md and DeskPhone-Standards.md that explicitly point future work at Signal Desktop, Element, Telegram Desktop, Linphone Desktop, and Jami before inventing or omitting baseline communication-app behavior.

notes: The dialer now stays anchored beside Recents again, recent-call rows expose inline message and callback actions with direct double-click callback, message bubbles expose copy, forward, and message pin actions in place, and scrolling is back to a clean conventional pill bar.
devNotes: Forced the Calls surface to stay split so the dialer no longer drops below the page, removed the oversized selected-call action card, and moved call and message actions into the recent-call rows on both Calls and Messages while wiring row double-click to direct callback. Added per-message quick-action tray state plus durable message pinning so bubble click and context affordances can copy, forward, or pin a specific message in place, restyled the scrollbar templates back into conventional trackless pills, and updated DeskPhone-Standards so mainstream phone-app interaction patterns are now an explicit repo baseline rather than tribal memory.

notes: Conversations can now be pinned to the top of the list, and individual threads can mute their own message alerts without hiding the thread itself or losing unread state.
devNotes: Added durable per-conversation preference storage in AppSettingsService for pinned threads and muted-alert threads, extended Conversation plus MainViewModel with those states and toggle commands, and changed conversation rebuild ordering so pinned threads stay grouped above the normal recency list. Updated the messages surface so the conversation list and selected-thread header both show the new state clearly, and routed incoming-message banner plus tray-chime decisions through the muted-thread setting so message sync still lands normally while interruptions are suppressed for opted-out threads.

notes: Recent calls now open a standard detail-and-actions view with obvious callback and message options, and dialer-side search results now show explicit Text and Call actions instead of auto-calling on contact click.
devNotes: Added selected recent-call state in MainViewModel so the Calls surface can show a proper detail card with callback, message, save-contact, and edit-contact actions for the chosen record. Updated the recents list to bind selection and highlight the active row, taught call-record display text to use formatted numbers consistently, and replaced the dialer contact-suggestion full-row call button with explicit Text and Call actions that reuse the existing message-target and direct-dial paths.

notes: The left rail can now collapse to an icon-only mode, the dialer stays compact instead of sprawling across the page, and vertical scrolling now uses slim theme-coordinated pill scrollbars instead of the default Windows blocks.
devNotes: Added a persisted navigation-rail collapse preference in AppSettingsService and MainViewModel, then wired MainWindow shell layout plus the rail markup so labels, footer content, and the splitter all respond cleanly when the user toggles icon-only mode. Reworked the dialer pane to use a centered compact content width with tighter keypad and action spacing, reduced the fixed dialer column size in split view, and replaced the old default scrollbar chrome with custom thumb and track templates that match the app palette.

notes: Contacts now lives as a real top-level sidebar destination, the conversation edit-contact pen opens the proper manager, and saved contacts can be searched, texted, called, edited, or added from one place.
devNotes: Added a dedicated Contacts tab with left-rail navigation sync so command-driven tab changes and sidebar clicks stay in agreement. Edit-contact flows from conversation, compose, and dialer now route into that manager, the old duplicate Contacts editor was removed from Settings, the saved-contact list now shows clear selection state, and contact actions are wired so Text jumps into the right message target while Call uses the existing direct-dial path instead of only staging a number.

notes: Message bubbles now stay inside the thread when the conversation lane is squeezed harder, with tighter bubble margins, slightly smaller bubble text only at narrow widths, and a sane floor so the pane stops before wrapping turns absurd.
devNotes: Reworked BubbleWidthConverter so the bubble cap always respects the actual visible thread width instead of enforcing a fixed floor that could exceed the lane and push past the right edge. Added a bubble-only text-size converter for narrow widths, reduced bubble padding and outer margins, and gave ConversationMessagesColumn a real minimum width so the thread stops compressing before it collapses into one-letter rows while leaving the rest of the app's text scale unchanged.

notes: Incoming message alerts now stay visible longer, show a fuller preview, open the correct conversation when clicked, and give clear Open and Reply actions instead of behaving like a dead notification.
devNotes: Extended the tray balloon timing, added a dedicated in-app message banner overlay in MainWindow, and finished the message-target routing path that the tray click handlers were already trying to call. MainViewModel now raises a richer per-message banner payload during both refresh and live delta sync, trims only very large message bodies for stability, and exposes helpers so the banner can jump into the exact conversation or straight into a reply path without disturbing the rest of the shell layout.

notes: Message bubbles now fit the visible thread width at larger text sizes instead of getting chopped at the frame edge, and the text-size slider now stays stable while you adjust it.
devNotes: Replaced the fixed-width SMS bubble caps with a thread-width-based converter so the message surface behaves more like a standard phone conversation view as the pane size changes. Also split the zoom control into a draft value plus release-to-apply commit path, then fed the responsive layout thresholds from the effective zoomed width so the shell stops reflowing under the cursor while the slider is being dragged.

notes: DeskPhone now has a real text-size and zoom control in Appearance, so you can enlarge the interface and the detached Live Log immediately instead of being stuck with tiny text.
devNotes: Added a persisted UI scale setting to AppSettingsService, exposed it through MainViewModel as a clamped zoom percentage, and applied that scale through layout transforms to both the main DeskPhone shell and the separate Live Log window. The Appearance section now includes a zoom card with a slider, live percentage badge, and reset action, so readability changes take effect immediately and survive restart without creating one-off font exceptions across individual screens.

notes: The live log now behaves like a normal separate window you open only when you need it, with a sidebar launch link under Changelog instead of an always-attached utility panel.
devNotes: Changed the log shell from an owned companion window into a standalone top-level window that opens on demand, appears in the taskbar, and can be closed independently without keeping itself glued above the main DeskPhone surface. MainWindow now launches or focuses that window from a dedicated sidebar link, defaults the log closed on startup, removes the old Appearance toggle, and explicitly closes the log during app shutdown so the separate window does not keep the process alive after the main shell exits.

notes: DeskPhone now keeps more calls from falling back to `Unknown` by matching live caller IDs and phone-synced names against saved contacts even when some imported contact numbers include extra dial metadata.
devNotes: Expanded contact-number interpretation in ContactStoreService so DeskPhone strips URI-style phone metadata for new imports and can score likely matches against older overlong numbers that were already saved locally. MainViewModel now uses that stronger matching path for live call banners, PBAP caller-name backfill, selected-conversation call history, and merged call-record refreshes, which lets exact contact matches still win while rescuing caller-name lookups that previously failed on conference-tail or metadata-tailed contact numbers.

notes: Settings now includes a real messages backup export action so you can save the current DeskPhone message history to a JSON file anywhere you choose without touching the app's automatic safety backups.
devNotes: Added a dedicated Message Backup Export card to the Settings sync area, exposed a new export command and status text in MainViewModel, and routed the save flow through a SaveFileDialog so the user chooses the destination file directly. MessageStoreService now shares its JSON writer between the live store path and ad-hoc exports, which keeps the on-demand backup format consistent with the app's existing message store while avoiding duplicate serialization logic.

notes: Call history now supports deleting individual entries, deleting the whole log with a warning, and keeping locally deleted phone-synced call entries from popping right back into DeskPhone on the next import.
devNotes: Added explicit call-log delete commands to both the Recents surface and the right-side call-history pane, including a warning-gated delete-all flow. Introduced a per-device call-log sync outbox that queues delete and delete-all operations for any real helper path that can apply them on the phone, while PBAP cache import now replaces stale device snapshots instead of only appending and respects pending local delete operations so phone-side removals drop out of the desktop cache and local desktop deletes stay suppressed until the handset catches up.

notes: The live debug log now opens in its own separate window, and the phone history beside an open conversation now stays as a permanent right-side vertical pane instead of dropping below the thread.
devNotes: Removed the root-shell live-log sidebar from MainWindow, introduced a dedicated LogWindow owned by the main shell and driven by the existing ShowLiveLog toggle, and repositioned it as a companion utility window with its own clear action and auto-scroll behavior. On the message surface, the conversation call-history area now stays anchored as the right-side detail pane at all supported widths, with the hide-call-history affordances removed so the layout no longer collapses into a bottom stack after the live log leaves the main surface.

notes: Incoming calls now show a resolved caller name when DeskPhone already knows one from contacts or phone-synced call history, and PBAP call-log names now auto-create or refresh matching local contacts instead of staying trapped only in the phone-log cache.
devNotes: Added a richer caller-identity path in MainViewModel that prefers stored contacts, falls back to phone-synced call-history names, and uses that result for the live incoming-call banner as well as contact-backed call-history refreshes. Also taught the PBAP call-log load and import paths to harvest useful caller names into the local contact store when they are missing or only number-shaped placeholders, while preserving the rule that PBAP itself remains read-only and does not pretend to write anything back to the handset.

notes: Contact add and edit actions now route into the real Contacts editor, contacts can be deleted directly from DeskPhone, and local contact changes now participate in an honest per-phone sync queue where a phone-side helper exists.
devNotes: Rewired the title-bar and number-driven contact actions so they open the Contacts editor inside Settings instead of behaving like dead buttons, added a real delete flow with confirmation, and extended contact sync from inbound VCF import only into a file-based per-device operation contract that can queue upsert and delete requests alongside inbound operation files. The sync status card and logs now explain that PBAP remains read-only for contacts, so phone-side parity depends on a real helper consuming the outbound queue rather than pretending Bluetooth wrote contacts back directly.

notes: Message sync no longer bogs down the Bluetooth lane by doing a full-phone delete sweep during normal live operation, and the delta-sync logging now clearly distinguishes cached local handles from real phone traffic.
devNotes: Added a dedicated message-sync gate so poll cycles, manual refreshes, and send follow-up refreshes no longer overlap on the MAP lane. Automatic delete reconciliation now compares only a recent inbox-and-sent handle window during live operation, while explicit manual refresh still retains a deeper full-history reconcile path. Also clarified the misleading handle-seed logging by separating bulk local-cache seeding from single-handle registration after a successful send, and kept the paused contact-management work out of this release.

notes: Deleting a message from DeskPhone now deletes the phone-backed message on the handset too, and the app now removes message history that the phone has already deleted instead of leaving stale copies behind on the PC.
devNotes: Extended the MAP layer with deletedStatus support through SetMessageStatus so DeskPhone can request a true phone-side message delete rather than only removing the item locally. Added a full inbox-and-sent handle reconciliation pass to the message sync loop and manual refresh path so the desktop store periodically compares itself against the phone's current message listings and prunes messages that no longer exist on the handset, even when the newest-handle probe does not change. Also expanded docs/DeskPhone-Standards.md to explicitly treat add, edit, delete, save, export, and sync parity as baseline communication-app behavior subject to Bluetooth profile boundaries.

notes: The message history thread now keeps the same smooth swipe-and-coast behavior as the other panes instead of losing momentum when a finger drag starts on top of a message body.
devNotes: Applied a message-thread-specific touch follow-up by explicitly setting the outer message ListBox to pixel-based vertical panning and disabling touch panning on the nested RichTextBox message-body surfaces. That keeps the conversation thread?s own scroll host in control of the gesture from start to finish, so inertial scrolling is no longer interrupted by the inner rich-text controls while link tapping and message rendering continue to work.

notes: Touch scrolling now feels smoother and more native on the main app surfaces, and maximized use no longer leaves the small transparent frame gaps around the shell edges on fullscreen-like layouts.
devNotes: Retuned the shared touch interaction layer in App.xaml by adding WPF panning deceleration, disabling press-and-hold and touch feedback behaviors that make swipes feel sticky, and switching ListBox virtualization to pixel scrolling so touch gestures move fluidly instead of stepping item-by-item. Also updated message bubble RichTextBox surfaces to stay readable and clickable without grabbing focus during a swipe, enabled layout rounding on the main window, and replaced the old hardcoded maximized-margin hack in MainWindow.xaml.cs with DPI-aware resize-border compensation so the shell fills the display edges consistently when maximized.

notes: Touch scrolling now works across the app?s main list and pane surfaces, so message history, call history, settings, changelog, and search suggestion panels can all be finger-scrolled on touch hardware instead of relying on mouse-wheel behavior.
devNotes: Added shared touch-panning styles in App.xaml for ScrollViewer and ListBox, enabled panning directly on the custom TextBox content host and ComboBox dropdown scroller, and updated the keyed suggestion-list and tab-level scrollviewer styles in MainWindow to inherit that touch behavior instead of overriding it. The result is a framework-level touch pass that covers the major pane and field surfaces without changing view-model logic or reshaping the app?s existing layout architecture.

notes: Settings now has a real dark-mode switch, and DeskPhone saves that choice explicitly so the selected light or dark mode comes back predictably after restart instead of being inferred loosely from palette state.
devNotes: Added an explicit persisted DarkModeEnabled setting to AppSettingsService, normalized older settings.json files by deriving that flag from the legacy PreferredPalette when needed, and centralized mode changes through SetDarkMode so dark-mode state and the BlueGold or Google palette stay in sync on disk. In MainViewModel, replaced the old palette-name inference with a direct IsDarkModeEnabled binding plus a clearer status label, and updated the Appearance settings card to use a dedicated switch-style ToggleButton so dark mode is exposed as a real on or off setting rather than a content-swapping pill.

notes: BlueGold now applies consistently across the app instead of leaving some shared controls and guidance cards stuck in the old light-theme colors after a theme change or restart.
devNotes: Removed the hardcoded Google palette dependency from Themes/Styles.xaml by dropping its local Colors.xaml merge and converting theme-token brush lookups in the shared button and dial-key styles from StaticResource to DynamicResource, so those controls now resolve against the live palette dictionary that ThemeService swaps. Converted the remaining app-level shared styles in App.xaml to dynamic palette lookups as well, including card, nav, textbox, combobox, caret, selection, border, and shadow resources, and replaced the leftover hardcoded light-theme reconnect, contact-import, pairing-guidance, and PBAP-guidance banner colors in MainWindow with palette-aware accent surfaces so BlueGold no longer shows white or Google-green patches.

notes: DeskPhone now tells you whether PBAP call-log sync is actually connected, missing on the phone, blocked by phone-side sharing permissions, or dropped mid-connect, and the Sync settings card shows a concrete next step instead of only saying Unavailable.
devNotes: Reworked PBAP status handling from string-matching into a typed availability model shared between PbapService and MainViewModel, so PBAP connection and import paths now classify unsupported or hidden PBAP exposure, phone-side permission refusals, dropped Bluetooth sessions, and post-connect import failures separately. Extended ObexClient to preserve OBEX GET response codes, taught PbapService to map RFCOMM and OBEX failures into explicit PBAP availability kinds plus user guidance, and updated the Sync settings PBAP card to show a single status badge with a targeted guidance panel and Bluetooth-settings shortcut whenever the phone needs user action.

notes: DeskPhone now folds the imported PBAP phone logs into the main call history surface instead of keeping them as a separate cache, and duplicate phone-log entries are collapsed cleanly instead of stacking up.
devNotes: Extended CallRecord with phone-sync metadata and taught MainViewModel to load the persisted PBAP call-log cache into CallHistory during startup, map PBAP records into the existing call-history model, and merge repeated PBAP imports back into the same history items instead of re-adding them. Added merge logic that matches PBAP entries against existing history by normalized number, direction, and close call-time windows, preserves imported timestamps while letting live-call duration enrich synced records, and logs the resulting insert or match counts so the merged-history behavior is verifiable from the running app.

notes: DeskPhone now downloads the phone?s incoming, outgoing, and missed PBAP call-history books into a local cache, so the app has real phone-side call-log data on disk instead of only live call events.
devNotes: Extended PbapService from session verification into actual PBAP data import by reopening the PBAP session, navigating to the telecom repository, downloading ich.vcf, och.vcf, and mch.vcf via OBEX GET, parsing each returned vCard block into normalized PBAP call-log entries, and surfacing import totals in the debug log and PBAP status text. Added a new PbapCallLogEntry model plus PbapCallLogStoreService to persist the imported phone-side call logs under %APPDATA%\DeskPhone\pbap-calllogs.json, dedupe repeat imports by direction, normalized number, and PBAP timestamp, and keep the cache sorted for the next build where that cache will be merged into the main call history surface.

notes: DeskPhone now goes beyond PBAP detection and proves it can open a real phonebook-access session with the phone, so call-log sync is standing on a verified transport instead of a guessed one.
devNotes: Replaced the PBAP readiness-only probe with a real PbapService that reuses the existing BluetoothDeviceConnector and OBEX stack to open the PBAP Phone Book Server RFCOMM channel, perform an OBEX CONNECT against the PBAP target UUID, then cleanly disconnect after a successful handshake. Generalized ObexClient so services can supply their own OBEX target instead of hardcoding MAP, updated MainViewModel to run the PBAP session check after HFP comes up, log the PBAP outcome directly into the debug log, and expose the result through the Settings card, which now reports PBAP connection rather than only PBAP readiness.

notes: The app now checks whether the connected phone actually advertises PBAP, and Settings shows a clear readiness state for future phone-side call-log sync instead of leaving that capability unknown.
devNotes: Added a dedicated PbapCapabilityService that first forces a stable Bluetooth link through the existing BluetoothDeviceConnector path, then performs uncached RFCOMM service discovery against the PBAP Phone Book Server UUID (0x112F). MainViewModel now runs that PBAP probe after a successful calls connection, logs the discovery details into the live debug log, and exposes a PBAP status string plus ready or pending state flags for the UI. The Sync settings section now includes a separate PBAP readiness card so we can tell immediately whether the current phone is eligible for later PBAP-backed call-log import work or if the phone simply does not expose the profile.

notes: Hands-free call audio setup now binds HFP service registration to the actual Bluetooth radio that owns the paired phone, which fixes the failed driver-registration path that was leaving call audio stuck on the handset.
devNotes: Reworked NativeBluetoothHelper.EnableHfpForDevice so it no longer enumerates the phone once with a null radio handle and then calls BluetoothSetServiceState against an unrelated radio context. The helper now iterates local radios with BluetoothFindFirstRadio and BluetoothFindNextRadio, enumerates each radio's paired devices using that same radio handle, and only calls BluetoothSetServiceState after finding the target address on the matching radio-bound BLUETOOTH_DEVICE_INFO record. Added the missing BluetoothFindNextRadio P/Invoke, tightened the success and failure descriptions around radio-scoped enumeration, and kept the existing dual GUID registration attempt for both Audio Gateway and Hands-Free Unit service classes.

notes: The main rail connection area now shows an explicit reconnect icon plus separate Calls and Messages status lines, so connection state is clearer and a clean reconnect to the saved phone stays one click away.
devNotes: Extended the main rail connection surface to expose channel-specific state instead of only a single blended status pill. MainViewModel now derives CallsConnectionLabel and MessagesConnectionLabel from the live HFP and MAP status strings, tracks whether quick reconnect is currently allowed, and keeps that state updated as connection and saved-device status changes. MainWindow now renders those per-channel labels directly under the main status line and adds a compact reconnect icon button bound to the existing clean reconnect path, which still routes through ConnectToAddressAsync and ResetConnectionSessionAsync for a full disconnect and reconnect to the same saved device.

notes: Settings now uses a real left-category selector with section detail on the right, the main rail exposes reconnect and connection-settings access directly, and maximized mode fills the side and bottom edges without the extra transparent frame gap.
devNotes: Replaced the one-long-page Settings layout with a two-pane settings shell in MainWindow: a left navigation column with Connection, Appearance, Contacts, Contact Sync, and Audio categories, plus section-specific content on the right driven by a new SettingsSection state model in MainViewModel. Added a persistent connection action area to the main navigation rail that shows connection state, quick-reconnect context for the preferred saved device, and a direct route into the Connection settings section. In MainWindow code-behind, added maximized-window frame compensation on the root shell grid so the client content covers the small side and bottom non-client gap that was still visible in fullscreen-like maximized use.

notes: Settings now opens with a dedicated connection hub at the top, gives fig-newton or your primary saved phone a one-click quick-connect spot, separates other saved devices below it, and moves the theme toggle into a new appearance section.
devNotes: Reworked the Settings tab structure in MainWindow so Connection is the first section, with a new quick-connect card bound to a prioritized saved-device model instead of burying reconnect inside the older generic devices card. MainViewModel now computes a preferred quick-connect device by favoring fig-newton-style names before falling back to the most recently seen saved device, exposes a separate OtherKnownDevices list for the rest of the saved phones, and refreshes those projections whenever the saved-device list changes. AppSettingsService now persists a PreferredPalette value, MainViewModel applies that saved palette on startup, and the new Settings appearance card binds a dark-mode toggle that switches between the BlueGold and Google palettes without scattering theme controls elsewhere in the shell.

notes: The new main-screen pane close and reopen controls now respond immediately instead of waiting for the window to be resized.
devNotes: The prior b82 pane controls were updating MainViewModel state correctly, but MainWindow only recomputed responsive layout on load, resize, move, or state change, so the UI did not visually react when those commands fired. MainWindow now listens for ShowMessagesListPane, ShowConversationCallHistoryPane, ShowRecentCallsPane, ShowDialerPane, and ShowLiveLog property changes from MainViewModel and re-runs ApplyResponsiveLayout on the dispatcher as soon as those flags change. This preserves the b82 pane-state model and command wiring while fixing the missing visual refresh path.

notes: The main screen now opens on the newest conversation by default, and the Messages and Calls surfaces both support closing side panes with on-surface reopen controls so history views stay accessible without relying on the sidebar.
devNotes: Added explicit pane-visibility state to MainViewModel for the Messages thread list, selected-conversation call-history pane, Calls recents pane, and dialer pane, along with matching open and close commands. MainWindow now renders compact hide buttons in each pane header plus persistent on-surface reopen chips when a pane is hidden, and the responsive layout controller collapses hidden panes by zeroing the affected columns or rows so the remaining content expands cleanly instead of leaving dead space. Conversation rebuilding now auto-selects the newest thread when no compose flow is active, which makes the main Messages surface show real text and call history immediately on launch.

notes: Added an in-repo agent handbook so future builds follow the app's Windows, Bluetooth, layout, and release standards without needing the same coaching each time.
devNotes: Created AGENTS.md as the repo entry point for future agents and added docs/DeskPhone-Standards.md to codify product-specific UI, layout, notification, pane, Bluetooth-profile, and deployment rules. Added docs/Release-Checklist.md to make the existing release discipline explicit, including changelog quality, Release-only shipping, previous-build preservation, update handoff behavior, and rewindable commit expectations. Updated deploy.ps1's staged release path list so AGENTS.md and the docs folder are included in future rewindable release commits.

notes: The app now clamps itself to the active screen, drops the log panel when space is tight, and reflows message and call layouts into stacked panes instead of overflowing fixed side columns.
devNotes: Lowered the hard window minimums, added runtime work-area clamping in MainWindow so the normal window cannot extend beyond the current monitor, and made the navigation rail shrink slightly on narrower widths. Reworked the message thread and Calls tab layouts into responsive two-mode grids: wide windows keep the side-by-side panes, while narrower widths move call history or the dialer into a stacked split layout with a horizontal splitter instead of forcing fixed-width overflow. Removed the live log textbox's 2000px minimum width and now auto-collapse the log sidebar when the shell width cannot support it cleanly.

notes: Outgoing sends no longer trigger redundant push notifications, sent bubbles now show a quiet in-thread sent confirmation, and the desktop shortcuts now use larger custom icons for latest and previous builds.
devNotes: Filtered the message-notification path so tray balloons only fire for newly synced incoming messages, not for messages the user just sent from DeskPhone. Updated the outgoing message bubble template to add a small Sent confirmation line inside the thread history pane without adding another badge or popup. Reworked the desktop shortcut icon assets into dedicated multi-size .ico files with tighter icon-safe composition, retargeted the live DeskPhone and DeskPhone Previous Build shortcuts to those icons, and expanded deploy.ps1's staged release path list so Assets are included in future release commits.

notes: Release deploys now offer the new build to the currently running app instead of force-opening it, with a deferred reminder indicator if you choose Not Yet.
devNotes: Added a deploy offer endpoint to the local control API, a one-shot pending build handoff record in settings, and a new in-app update prompt plus reminder indicator. When the user accepts, the running build persists the archived EXE path and connected device, shuts itself down cleanly, and launches the new build after a short delay. On startup, the accepted build consumes that handoff, resets any local session state, and performs a clean reconnect to the device that had been connected in the prior build. deploy.ps1 now posts the update offer to a running DeskPhone instance and only auto-launches the new EXE when no build is already running.

notes: Release deploys now preserve the prior runnable build, publish a stable latest shortcut, and always create a separate previous-build shortcut for immediate fallback.
devNotes: deploy.ps1 now archives each Release output into a versioned deployed-builds folder instead of launching directly from bin\Release, so the prior shipped build survives even if later builds clean the normal output directory. The script no longer shuts down the previous running DeskPhone instance during deploy, updates a durable DeskPhone desktop shortcut to the latest archived EXE, and keeps a DeskPhone Previous Build shortcut pointed at the most recent earlier archived build.

notes: Hang-up now cancels stuck dialing or ringing attempts reliably, and Release deploys now require a detailed changelog plus an automatic rewindable Git commit.
devNotes: Updated HfpService so Hang Up moves a live or pending call into Ending, sends AT+CHUP, and force-clears the local call state after a guarded timeout if the phone never reports the final idle indicator. MainViewModel command wiring now routes Answer, Dial, and Hang Up through the view-model wrappers consistently. Reworked deploy.ps1 to reject non-Release builds, require a prewritten detailed changelog entry for the current build number, and create a Git commit from the curated source tree before launch so each deployed build is easy to roll back.

notes: Build 74 automated deployment.
Messages now keep the full call history visible beside the text thread, contact search uses live dropdown suggestions, and the app now opens in a deep blue-and-gold dark theme.
devNotes: Removed the per-contact recent-call strip from the thread header and replaced it with a full call-history pane inside the Messages view so call and text history stay visible together without switching tabs. The compose and dialer contact pickers now use typed suggestion dropdowns instead of fixed contact lists; compose clicks select a recipient, while dialer clicks place the call immediately. Added a new BlueGold palette and made it the default startup palette so the app boots into the requested deep blue and gold dark mode.

notes: Build 73 automated deployment.
Trimmed the thread header clutter, made contact actions smarter, kept call and text history together, and sped up opening large message threads.
devNotes: Removed the always-on contact sync banner from the main thread view, moved pending-contact import actions into Settings, swapped the conversation action pills for compact icon buttons, and now show Add Contact only for unknown numbers while saved matches route to Edit Contact. The dialer pane now scrolls safely on short windows, uses smaller controls, and double-clicking a searched contact places the call. The message thread body also moved to a virtualized ListBox, and selecting an already-read conversation now skips the unnecessary full-message save path that was adding click latency.

notes: Read and unread now behave like a phone conversation: sent messages stay read, opening or marking a thread here pushes read state back to the phone, and recent phone-side read changes sync back into the app.
devNotes: Added MAP SetMessageStatus write-back in MapService using x-bt/messageStatus plus StatusIndicator/StatusValue app parameters, then updated MainViewModel so Mark Read and Mark Unread only target incoming messages while forcing sent items to remain read. The poll and manual refresh flows now also pull a recent listing-only metadata window and patch local IsRead values by handle so phone-side read changes come back into the app without re-downloading message bodies.

notes: Conversation unread badges now default old saved history to read, and current unread state follows the phone's message read flags.
devNotes: Added a legacy-message-store migration in MessageStoreService so old histories without reliable IsRead data load as read, then changed conversation unread logic to derive from SmsMessage.IsRead coming from MAP metadata instead of a separate local readstate file. Manual Mark Read and Mark Unread now update the local message store directly.

notes: Build 70 automated deployment.
Cleaned up the Calls contact picker, restored visible unread state, and added recent-call plus sync status context directly into the main conversation pane.
devNotes: Removed the hard contact result cap in MainViewModel, replaced the Calls tab 'Use' pill rows with direct list selection, reconnected ReadStateService-backed unread tracking, and added selected-conversation call history/sync summary bindings in the main detail pane without replacing the existing message thread flow.
